Sign up for my newsletter or join my WhatsApp Group to keep up with my latest work in the community and in Westminster. pages.title: HELP FOR CONSTITUENTS | Liam Byrne MP
headings: h2: LIAM'S COST OF LIVING GUIDE
h2: Since becoming your MP, Liam has been tirelessly fighting for your fair share. See below what Liam has achieved over the years of his Hodge Hill journey.
pages.text_excerpt: --> HELP FOR CONSTITUENTS | Liam Byrne MP top of page HOME HELP FOR CONSTITUENTS RAISE A LOCAL ISSUE YOUR AREA CAMPAIGNS - HUMAN RIGHTS ABOUT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. SURVEY HELP FOR CONSTITUENTS If you need help with any issues ranging from immigration to benefits, please do not hesitate to contact me. I receive hundreds of emails a month from constituents and my casework team and I will do everything we can to advocate for your fair share, no matter the case at hand. ​ Below are a series of links and contact details for various organisations across the constituency, or online resources, which may be able to help you or answer any questions you have. To raise an issue with me, please email liam.byrne.mp@parliament.uk including your name, address, details of your case and any further information or reference numbers that may be relevant to your case. MPs can only take on the cases of those within their constituency. Therefore, please do not contact me unless you are a constituent in Birmingham Hodge Hill. ​ ​ CLICK HERE TO CHECK WHO YOUR MP IS SURGERIES Liam holds monthly surgeries at Ward End Fire Station, Elmwood Hub, and at his office in Hodge Hill. To book an appointment email liam.byrne.mp@parliament.uk or call 0121789 7287 SCHEDULE A MEETING HELPFUL ORGANISATIONS Birmingham City Council Advice Centre, 57 Sutton New Road, B23 6QT +44 (0)121 216 3030 Visit website Citizens' Advice Ground Floor Gazette Buildings, 168 Corporation Street, B4 6TF 0344 477 1010 Visit website Welcome Change Welcome Change Community Hub, 316 Kitts Green Road, B33 9SB +44 (0)121 216 3030 Visit website The Dolphin Centre Ralph Road, Saltley, B8 1NA +44 (0)121 326 8343 The Pump 286 Kitts Green Road, Glebe Farm B33 9SB, +44 (0)121 675 8381 Visit website Visit website Screenshot_20230330-100859_Drive Screenshot_20230330-100921_Drive Screenshot_20230330-101104_Drive Screenshot_20230330-100859_Drive 1/8 LIAM'S COST OF LIVING GUIDE Since becoming your MP, Liam has been tirelessly fighting for your fair share. See below what Liam has achieved over the years of his Hodge Hill journey. LIAM'S HODGE HILL RECORD Hodge Hill is home to some wonderful community projects and we want to support all groups to keep making a difference in our community. ​ Our flagship event is our annual Hodge Hill Funders Fair where we invite major funders along to share with you the type of projects they fund and what criteria need to be met to ensure your bid is successful. Last year’s fair was held on 31st July at Saltley Wellbeing Community Centre and was attended by; Asda, Birmingham Airport, Groundwork West Midlands, National Lottery Heritage Fund, Severn Trent, Sport Birmingham, Street Games, Tesco, Veolia Environmental Trust and West Midlands Community Housing. CLICK HERE TO SEE MORE ABOUT FUNDING OUT COMMUNITY GROUPS FUNDING COMMUNITY GROUPS LIAM BYRNE MP Email: liam.byrne.mp@parliament.uk Phone: 0121 789 7287 Follow Liam on Social Media: Privacy Policy > Acceptable Behaviour Policy > © 2024, The 1900 Creative LTD . headings: h2: Royal Mail boss hauled before Business and Trade Committee
pages.text_excerpt: --> YOUR AREA | Liam Byrne MP top of page HOME HELP FOR CONSTITUENTS RAISE A LOCAL ISSUE YOUR AREA CAMPAIGNS - HUMAN RIGHTS ABOUT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. SURVEY LATEST NEWS All Posts BROMFORD AND HODGE HILL CASTLE BROMWICH GARRETTS GREEN GLEBE FARM AND TILE CROSS HEARTLANDS SHARD END SMITH'S WOOD WARD END Press Release foreign policy Publications Economics Speeches In the Constituency In Parliament Byrne After-Reading NEWS Royal Mail boss hauled before Business and Trade Committee Our post is broken. And our community is suffering for it. Missing hospital letters. Lost prescriptions. Court documents going astray. I've been hearing it on doorsteps right across the constituency. So, the Commons Business and Trade Committee, which I chair, hauled in the Royal Mail's new owner. He’s on notice. He has committed to publishing an improvement plan. I'll be watching - and reporting every step back to you. Read my column in the latest Solihull Observer:... Liam Byrne MP Apr 7 SMALL BUSINESS STRATEGY PUBLISHED Small businesses across Hodge Hill and Solihull North, across the country, are facing pandemic-level pressures but without the emergency support. The Business and Trade Committee report published today exposes: £112bn owed in unpaid invoices 38 stores closing on high streets every day Energy costs nearly doubled since 2021 Crime adding 10p to every shop transaction. Local cafés need to sell hundreds more items monthly just to survive. Hair salons face £25,000 extra costs. Liam Byrne MP Feb 12 Pavement Parking Update 📢 Update on Pavement Parking Following up on the pavement parking video I shared earlier this week, I’ve now received a written response from the Transport Minister, Lillian Greenwood MP, which you can read below 👇 Why this matters: In Hodge Hill and Solihull North, blocked pavements aren’t a nuisance — they’re a daily barrier for disabled residents, older people, and parents with pushchairs. What the Minister has confirmed: • New powers for councils to tackle pavement ob Liam Byrne MP Jan 29 1 2 3 4 5 Follow us on X (Twitter) LIAM BYRNE MP Email: liam.byrne.mp@parliament.uk Phone: 0121 789 7287 Follow Liam on Social Media: Privacy Policy > Acceptable Behaviour Policy > © 2024, The 1900 Creative LTD . All rights reserved. bottom of page

pages.title: ABOUT | Liam Byrne MP
headings: h2: NEED HELP WITH A LOCAL ISSUE?
pages.text_excerpt: --> ABOUT | Liam Byrne MP top of page HOME HELP FOR CONSTITUENTS RAISE A LOCAL ISSUE YOUR AREA CAMPAIGNS - HUMAN RIGHTS ABOUT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. SURVEY ABOUT LIAM Liam is the Labour MP for Birmingham Hodge Hill and Solihull North, a member of His Majesty's Privy Council, elected Chair of the Global Parliamentary Network on the World Bank and IMF and Chair of the House of Commons' Business and Trade Select Committee. As Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster during the Great Financial Crisis, Liam coordinated No 10 Downing Street for Gordon Brown before serving as Chief Secretary to Her Majesty's Treasury. He is a former member of the Council of Europe and is one of Westminster's leading authorities on China. An economic historian, Liam has published over twenty books, pamphlets and chapters on economic history, foreign policy and public service reform. Liam's history of British capitalism, Dragons was published in 2016. Liam has written for Newsweek, the Financial Times, the Daily Mirror, the Guardian and New Statesman, and lectured at Harvard University, Oxford University and the LSE. ​ See a list of Liam's publications here . Liam graduated top of his year at Manchester University, before winning a Fulbright Scholarship to the Harvard Business School where he graduated with honours, later becoming a Gwilym Gibbon Research Fellow at Nuffield College, Oxford. Before entering politics, Liam worked in strategic consulting and banking, before founding a fast-growing venture-backed European technology company in 2000. He gave up his company in the 2004 by-election to serve Hodge Hill in East Birmingham, where five generations of his family have lived and worked. ​ Liam is married to Sarah, a graduate of Aston University. Together they have three children, Alex, John and Kyla. NEED HELP WITH A LOCAL ISSUE? My team and I are here to help in any way we can. Click the button below to raise an issue with us. GET IN TOUCH LIAM BYRNE MP Email: liam.byrne.mp@parliament.uk Phone: 0121 789 7287 Follow Liam on Social Media: Privacy Policy > Acceptable Behaviour Policy > © 2024, The 1900 Creative LTD . All rights reserved. bottom of page

headings: h2: £20 MILLION SECURED FOR GLEBE FARM INVESTMENT!
h2: HOW WOULD YOU USE THAT MONEY? TELL ME BELOW.
pages.text_excerpt: --> Glebe Farm Fund Survey | Liam Byrne MP top of page HOME HELP FOR CONSTITUENTS RAISE A LOCAL ISSUE YOUR AREA CAMPAIGNS - HUMAN RIGHTS ABOUT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. SURVEY £20 MILLION SECURED FOR GLEBE FARM INVESTMENT! HOW WOULD YOU USE THAT MONEY? TELL ME BELOW. On 25th September, we received the fantastic news that £20 million has been secured for Glebe Farm as part of the Government's Plan for Neighbourhoods funding scheme. This wouldn't have been possible without a massive collective effort by the constituents of Hodge Hill and Solihull North in signing my petition to Parliament to demand our fair share. Thank you all! Now, we need to decide how that money should be spent – and I want to hear how YOU would like that money used. Please tell me via the survey below. First name * Last name * Phone Email * Address * 1) What is the number 1 issue that you want me to prioritise to get fixed? Single choice Upgrade Glebe Farm Rec and outdoor gym Upgrade Tile Cross Park Help Welcome Change expand services at the new base of Glebe Farm Library Support The Pump's expansion of projects for young people Sort out parking on Glebe Farm and Farmcote Roads Tackle fly-tipping on Farmcote Road and Treaford, Cotterills, and Cole Hall Lanes Tackle speeding on Kitts Green, Audley, Flaxley, Wyndhurst and Tile Cross Roads Fix parking problems outside Audley School Tackle irresponsible parking outside Lea Forest Academy Improve road safety around Oval School 2) Have I missed any local issues that you need fixing? 2) Have I missed any local issues that you need fixing?
